meta.title: A Mutant Standard Virus Isolated from Vesicular Stomatitis Virus Persistent Infection Interferes Specifically with Wild-type Virus Replication

abstractHtml: A clone of vesicular stomatitis virus (VSV) isolated after 76 months of persistence exerts specific homologous interference with replication of wild-type or tsG31 (temperature-sensitive) virus in a mixed infection at 37 °C in the absence of defective-interfering particles. Other VSV ts mutants showed little or no specific interfering ability.
